definition: Value-growth spread at 5th-8th percentile post-1963 implies 400-700bp annualized value factor returns over 5-7 years, with no negative 5-year outcome in sample. Uniquely asymmetric: cheap quintile is non-distressed (financials, utilities, energy at 8-12x with stable cash flows) while expensive quintile is entirely AI narrative-driven.
evidence: No negative 5-year outcome in historical sample at these levels
Cheap quintile: financials, utilities, energy at 8-12x with stable cash flows
Historical 400-700bp annualized returns over 5-7 years from similar levels
open_questions: Timing unreliable: 1999-2000 spread widened further 12-18 months before reverting violently
Whether AI narrative moderation alone is sufficient catalyst or macro shock needed
